title: helianthus pauciflorus subsp. subrhomboideus
intro: Helianthus pauciflorus subsp. subrhomboideus, also known as the prairie sunflower, is a subspecies of the sunflower plant that is native to the prairies of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and other health issues. The health benefits of Helianthus pauciflorus subsp. subrhomboideus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
